ULTA BEAUTY, INC. AMENDED AND RESTATED 2011 INCENTIVE AWARD PLAN

RESTRICTED STOCK UNIT AWARD AGREEMENT – Performance Shares

Ulta Beauty, Inc.

d9c3d842-6bce-4a30-8682-8d948f9fd437.label: text_chunk
d9c3d842-6bce-4a30-8682-8d948f9fd437.text: (the “ Company ”) pursuant to the Ulta Beauty, Inc. Amended and Restated 2011 Incentive Award Plan (the “ Plan ”) hereby grants the following individual the right to earn Restricted Stock Units (each, an “ RSU ”), subject to the requirements set forth herein and in the Plan.  Each RSU earned entitles the Holder to receive an equal number of shares of common stock, par value $0.

83951334-c919-443c-971b-0a04033df550.label: text_chunk
83951334-c919-443c-971b-0a04033df550.text:
01 per share (“ Shares ”) at settlement, as described herein.Grant:
Name:
Grant Date
Target Number of RSUs Granted (“Target Award”)
Performance Conditions:
Performance Period:
[ ⚫ ] Earning of RSUs and Vesting Date:
The RSUs are earned (or not) and become “ Vesting Eligible RSUs ”) based on achievement relative to the Performance Goals and the formulas set forth on Exhibit A to this Award Agreement.

be7f4b02-2407-4c53-97b8-b841fa7cd23a.label: text_chunk
be7f4b02-2407-4c53-97b8-b841fa7cd23a.text: To the extent that the Performance Goals are met, then any Vesting Eligible RSUs (and any earned Dividend Equivalents thereon) shall be paid to the Holder on March 15, [ ⚫ ] (the “ Vesting Date ”), provided the Holder has not incurred a Termination of Service prior to the Vesting Date (except as otherwise provided in Exhibit A).Unless otherwise defined herein, capitalized terms shall have the same meanings set forth in the Plan.2

9a1a33d6-5e89-4309-849e-ce5cc4e9753a.label: text_chunk
9a1a33d6-5e89-4309-849e-ce5cc4e9753a.text: 1. Determination of Earned RSUs .  The number of RSUs granted, represents a target number of shares that may be earned based upon satisfaction of the target Performance Goal(s) as set forth on Exhibit A (the “ Target Award ”).

34d3b3cb-5798-491c-bab5-2705d931e2c4.label: text_chunk
34d3b3cb-5798-491c-bab5-2705d931e2c4.text: The actual number of RSUs earned (“ Vesting Eligible RSUs ”) may be greater or less than the Target Award, or even zero and will be determined based on the Company's actual performance level achieved according to the formulas set forth on Exhibit A.  All RSUs that do not become Vesting Eligible RSUs shall be forfeited.

d52fb5b8-c285-40e3-a8dc-b8df7cc3f1ae.label: text_chunk
d52fb5b8-c285-40e3-a8dc-b8df7cc3f1ae.text: Once the performance conditions for becoming Vesting Eligible RSUs are satisfied, such Vesting Eligible RSUs remain subject forfeiture until RSUs become “ Vested RSUs ” on the Vesting Date, unless otherwise provided on  Exhibit A.2. Limits on Transfer .

71ba85b7-d579-4281-b4ae-86342b386e96.label: text_chunk
71ba85b7-d579-4281-b4ae-86342b386e96.text: Holder may not sell, pledge, transfer, subject to lien, assign or otherwise hypothecate the RSUs unless and until the RSUs become Vested RSUs, and all other terms and conditions set forth herein and in the Plan have been satisfied.  Any attempt to do so contrary to the provisions of this Award Agreement shall be null and void.3. Non-Compete, Non-Solicitation and Confidential Information .

03b3035a-e12f-4747-bbe5-e3a704efa41f.label: text_chunk
03b3035a-e12f-4747-bbe5-e3a704efa41f.text:
The grant of the RSUs is subject to either Holder consenting to or having already consented to and abiding by the terms of the Confidential Information & Protective Covenants Agreement (the “ CIPCA ”).

4. Forfeiture .

18903c92-dd25-4875-a6ba-cc6a65846802.label: text_chunk
18903c92-dd25-4875-a6ba-cc6a65846802.text:
(a) Unless otherwise provided in Exhibit A, the RSUs shall be forfeited upon the Holder’s Termination of Service with the Company or if Holder violates the CIPCA prior to the Settlement Date, whether or not such RSUs were otherwise Vesting Eligible RSUs.

(b) Notwithstanding  Section  4(a),

72390db6-71b5-4cec-8485-ce67576f756c.label: text_chunk
72390db6-71b5-4cec-8485-ce67576f756c.text: if  the  Holder  has  a  Termination  of  Service  by  reason  of  the  Holder’s  Qualified  Retirement  prior  to  March  15, [ ⚫ ], the RSUs will remain outstanding and eligible to vest on March 15, [ ⚫ ] (and shall be settled in accordance with Section 5) as if the Holder has not  incurred  a  Termination  of  Service;  provided,

fa305382-a829-42b6-8489-054a99b1cd53.label: text_chunk
fa305382-a829-42b6-8489-054a99b1cd53.text: however,  that  if  the  Holder  owns,  operates,  or  provides  any  advisory,  employment,  director  or  other similar services to any Competitive Business (as defined in the CIPCA) in the Restricted Area (as defined in the CIPCA) at any time during the two (2) years  following  Holder's  Termination  of  Service,

04039779-2883-432f-99ca-59468683c220.label: text_chunk
04039779-2883-432f-99ca-59468683c220.text:
then  the  unvested  RSUs  will  be  immediately  forfeited.

    For  purposes  of  this Agreement  “ Qualified Retirement ” shall mean the Holder’s Termination of Service other than by the Company for Cause at such time that (x) the Holder has reached the age of 55, (y) the sum, rounded up to the nearest whole number,

0ca7cbc7-c88a-423a-b7c6-cb42212cf3c3.label: text_chunk
0ca7cbc7-c88a-423a-b7c6-cb42212cf3c3.text: of the Holder’s age (measured to two decimal points) and the number of years (measured to two decimal points) of uninterrupted service with the Company as an Employee, Consultant or Non-Employee Director, is greater than or equal to seventy (70), and (z) to the extent that the Termination of Service is a result of the Holder’s resignation from the Company,

b2ae267b-4efe-47c1-8745-9cf747fb3cb6.label: text_chunk
b2ae267b-4efe-47c1-8745-9cf747fb3cb6.text: the Holder has provided the Company with at least  one  (1)  year  prior  written  notice  of  the  Holder’s  intent  to  retire  (or  such  other  shorter  minimum  advance  written  notice  that  is  acceptable  to  the Administrator  in  its  sole  discretion),  provided  that  the  Holder  continues  to  provide  services  during  the  notice  period.

e27b2d05-8cd4-40a7-8f5a-231c2aeba756.label: text_chunk
e27b2d05-8cd4-40a7-8f5a-231c2aeba756.text:
The  determination  of  the Administrator as to an individual’s Qualified Retirement shall be conclusive on all parties.3
5. Settlement and Payment of RSUs .  Unless an earlier date is required in Exhibit A, the Company will deliver to Holder the number  of  Shares  equal  to  the  Vested  RSUs  as  on  the  Vesting  Date.

06f897c6-cc66-428a-ba07-de4dd8399adc.label: text_chunk
06f897c6-cc66-428a-ba07-de4dd8399adc.text: The  Company  shall  deliver  the  Shares  electronically  into  a  brokerage  account designated by Holder and shall not be required to deliver actual physical Share certificates.  The issuance of Shares in settlement of Vested RSUs will be subject to tax withholding, as provided below.6. Withholding .

9705d6ee-868a-4913-8af8-67d47f34919d.label: text_chunk
9705d6ee-868a-4913-8af8-67d47f34919d.text: The Company has the authority to deduct or withhold, or require Holder to remit to the Company, an amount sufficient to satisfy applicable federal, state, local and foreign withholding taxes with respect to the Shares issued in settlement of Vested RSUs.

6b7c9a1d-2bd4-4dbe-ae64-0f488889a043.label: text_chunk
6b7c9a1d-2bd4-4dbe-ae64-0f488889a043.text: A Holder may elect to satisfy his or her tax obligation, in whole or in part: (i) with the consent of the Company, by surrendering Shares or having the Company withhold Shares otherwise issuable under this Award Agreement, in each case with a Fair Market Value on the date of such surrender or withholding equal to the minimum amount of the tax withholding obligation or (ii) by payment in cash or check.

620c0fb0-c4e8-4b52-bd50-aa52c9ebbe71.label: text_chunk
620c0fb0-c4e8-4b52-bd50-aa52c9ebbe71.text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, if the Holder made no such election or the tax obligation arises during a period in which the Holder is prohibited from trading under any policy of the Company or by reason of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, then the tax withholding obligation shall automatically be satisfied by the Company withholding Shares having a Fair Market Value equal to the minimum amount of the tax withholding obligation.

feb2fb89-168f-48fd-a1e8-3bb6a9244a43.label: text_chunk
feb2fb89-168f-48fd-a1e8-3bb6a9244a43.text: No Shares will be delivered to Holder in settlement of vested RSUs under Section 5 unless and until all tax withholding obligations have been satisfied.7. Rights  as  Stockholder .    The  RSUs  awarded  under  this  Award  Agreement  do  not  confer  upon  Holder  any  rights  as  a stockholder, including but not limited to any right to vote or receive dividends.

943f7b55-4668-43c5-845e-d81e6bd79361.label: text_chunk
943f7b55-4668-43c5-845e-d81e6bd79361.text: To the extent that dividends are paid on Shares, Holder shall be entitled to receive with respect to the RSUs, dividend equivalent amounts equal to the regular cash dividend payable to holders of Shares (to the extent regular cash dividends are paid) as if Holder were an actual shareholder with respect to the number of Shares equal to his or her outstanding RSUs (the “ Dividend Equivalents ”).

a7f92649-50e7-44ec-99cc-113f31dd269b.label: text_chunk
a7f92649-50e7-44ec-99cc-113f31dd269b.text: Holder’s rights to Dividend Equivalents shall cease upon forfeiture or payment of the RSUs.  The aggregate amount of such Dividend Equivalents shall be held by the Company, without interest thereon, and paid to Holder as of the next payroll period after the RSUs are settled as provided in  Section  5.

17c65bf1-7afa-4e73-84ad-7b77e5952b25.label: text_chunk
17c65bf1-7afa-4e73-84ad-7b77e5952b25.text: Any  Dividend  Equivalents  held  by  the  Company  on  RSUs  which  do  not  become  Vested  RSUs  shall  be  forfeited  and  retained  by  the Company.8. Employment .  This Award Agreement does not constitute a contract of employment, and does not confer upon Holder the right to be retained in the employ of the Company or any Subsidiary.

abaf3cc0-e46b-4905-986f-67f608df285e.label: text_chunk
abaf3cc0-e46b-4905-986f-67f608df285e.text: In addition, nothing in the Plan or this Award Agreement shall be interpreted to interfere with or limit in any way the right of the Company to terminate Holder’s employment or services at any time.9. No Additional Rights .  Participation in the Plan is voluntary.

7852a5ee-0b7d-4db0-a2b8-fb9f80387dc8.label: text_chunk
7852a5ee-0b7d-4db0-a2b8-fb9f80387dc8.text:
The value of the RSUs is an extraordinary item that is not part of normal  or  expected  compensation  for  purposes  of  calculating  any  severance,  resignation,  redundancy,  end  of  service  payments,  bonuses,  long-service awards, pensions or retirement benefits or similar payments unless specifically and otherwise provided in such plans.  Rather, the awarding of the RSUs under the Plan represents a mere investment.4
10.

8a83e1b5-9547-4e8d-bbc5-2a590fcca844.label: text_chunk
8a83e1b5-9547-4e8d-bbc5-2a590fcca844.text: Limitations  on  Plan  Rights .    The  RSUs  are  granted  under  and  governed  by  the  terms  and  conditions  of  the  Plan.    By acceptance of the RSUs, Holder acknowledges and agrees that the Plan is discretionary in nature and may be amended, cancelled, or terminated by the Company, in its sole discretion, at any time.

50c30f0f-930d-47db-b12a-81eb42992638.label: text_chunk

The summary of the relationships is as follows:

1. **Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)**:
   - The SEC regulates the filing of **FORM 10-K**, which is a mandatory report that public companies must submit to disclose their financial performance. This regulation ensures transparency and accountability in the financial reporting of public entities.

2. **Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC)**:
   - The CPSC regulates consumer products and ensures compliance with the **Fair Packaging and Labeling Act**. This regulation mandates accurate labeling and packaging of consumer products, promoting consumer protection and informed purchasing decisions.

In summary, both the SEC and CPSC play crucial roles in regulating their respective domains—financial reporting for public companies and consumer product labeling—thereby safeguarding public interest and promoting transparency.

**Summary of Relationships Involving Merchandise Inventories and Accounts Payable**

1. **Entities Involved**: 
   - Merchandise Inventories

   - Working Capital Components
   - Cost of Sales

2. **Key Relationships**:
   - **Working Capital Significance**: Merchandise inventories and accounts payable are critical components of working capital. Inventories are assets held for sale, while accounts payable are liabilities representing amounts owed for those assets.
   - **Valuation**: As of January 28, 2023, merchandise inventories are valued at $1.6 billion, highlighting their substantial financial impact.
   - **Financing Mechanism**: Companies often finance their merchandise inventories through accounts payable, indicating that inventory purchases are frequently made on credit, creating obligations to suppliers.

This summary encapsulates the financial dynamics between merchandise inventories and accounts payable, emphasizing their roles in working capital management and cost accounting.

community_summary.257:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Product Offerings and Results of Operations**: Supporting product offerings through various channels can create operational issues that adversely affect financial performance.

2. **Guest-Facing Technology and Third-Party Vendors**: The development of guest-facing technology is influenced by third-party vendors, where delays can hinder implementation.

3. **Consumer Spending and Merchandise**: Changes in consumer spending directly impact merchandise levels, with lower spending leading to reduced demand.

4. **Market Share and Business**: A decrease in market share can reduce competitiveness and revenue, negatively affecting overall business operations and profitability.

5. **Reputation and Business**: A damaged reputation can lead to loss of customers and revenue, significantly impacting business success.

6. **Information Technology Systems and Financial Results**: The effectiveness and security of IT systems directly influence financial results, with failures potentially leading to financial losses.

7. **E-commerce Sales and Brand Reputation**: Damage to brand reputation can negatively impact e-commerce sales, as consumers may avoid brands with compliance issues.

8. **Privacy Laws and Brand Reputation**: Non-compliance with privacy laws can harm brand reputation, as consumers expect protection of their personal data.

9. **Selling Prices and Gross Margin**: Selling prices directly affect gross margin, with increases potentially leading to higher margins if costs remain stable.

10. **Income Tax and Net Income**: Income tax expenses are deducted from income, impacting the reported net income.

11. **Impairment, Restructuring, and Other Costs and Operating Income**: These costs are deducted from revenue to determine operating income, influencing profitability.

12. **Valuation Adjustments and Inventories**: Valuation adjustments are made when inventory costs exceed expected realizable amounts.

13. **Shrink Rate and Shrink Reserve**: The shrink rate influences the calculation of the shrink reserve, determining expected losses.

14. **Deferred Taxes and Income Tax Rates**: Changes in income tax rates affect the amount of deferred taxes recognized, altering tax liabilities.

15. **Redemption Activity and Redemption Rate**: The nature of redemption activity influences the redemption rate, measuring how often points are redeemed.

16. **Stock Options Exercised and Other Awards and Net Income**: The exercise of stock options can impact net income through additional expenses or changes in equity.

17. **Repurchase of Common Shares and Net Income**: Repurchasing common shares can influence net income by altering earnings per share and overall financial health.

18. **Rent Escalations and Total Rent Payable**: Rent escalations increase the total rent payable over the lease term.

19. **Stock Compensation and Effective Tax Rate**: Stock compensation can affect the effective tax rate by influencing taxable income and overall tax liability.

This summary encapsulates the interconnectedness of various business elements, illustrating how changes in one area can significantly impact others, particularly in terms of financial performance and operational efficiency.

community_summary.259:
The relationships outlined involve the concepts of deferred revenue, gift card sales revenue, and salon service revenue, highlighting the financial dynamics of service delivery and revenue recognition.

1. **Deferred Revenue and Gift Card Sales Revenue**: When a company sells gift cards, the revenue from these sales is initially classified as deferred revenue. This means that the revenue is not recognized until the gift card is redeemed by the customer for salon services.

2. **Salon Service Revenue**: Once the gift card is redeemed, the revenue is then recognized as salon service revenue. This indicates that the company acknowledges the income only at the point of service delivery, ensuring that revenue recognition aligns with the actual provision of services.

3. **Financial Obligation**: The concept of deferred revenue also signifies a financial obligation for the company. It indicates that the company has received payment for services that it is required to deliver in the future, reinforcing the idea that deferred revenue represents a liability until the service is rendered.

In summary, these relationships illustrate the process of revenue recognition in the context of gift card sales and service delivery, emphasizing the importance of timing in financial reporting and the obligations that arise from advance payments for services.
community_summary.260:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Economy and Consumer Purchases**: The economy significantly influences consumer purchases, with factors like inflation and employment levels affecting consumers' willingness to spend.

2. **Stock Repurchase Program and Company**: A company's stock repurchase program can impact its common stock market price, potentially leading to a decline if the program is suspended or discontinued.

3. **Distribution Capacity and Stores**: The distribution capacity of a company directly affects stores' ability to receive and sell products, which is crucial for overall business performance.

4. **Supply Disruption and Product Availability**: Supply disruptions can temporarily reduce product availability in stores, impacting sales and customer satisfaction.

5. **Licenses and Competitors**: The nature of licenses (exclusive or non-exclusive) affects competitors' access to intellectual property, influencing market competition dynamics.

6. **Relocated Stores and Merchandising**: The relocation of stores can necessitate changes in merchandising strategies, requiring different promotional tactics to attract customers.

7. **Contingent Rent and Lease Expense**: Contingent rent introduces variability in lease expenses based on the performance of leased assets, affecting the total costs incurred by lessees.

8. **Incremental Borrowing Rate and Modification**: Modifications to lease agreements can affect the incremental borrowing rate, leading to reassessments based on new terms.

9. **Diluted Net Income and Common Stock Equivalents**: Diluted net income is influenced by common stock equivalents, which can dilute earnings per share calculations.

10. **Tenant Incentives and Cash Flow**: Cash received from tenant incentives impacts overall cash flow, particularly concerning cash paid for operating lease liabilities.

11. **Federal and State Provisions for Income Taxes**: Both federal and state governments impose income taxes, directly influencing the provision for income taxes based on their respective regulations.

12. **Common Stock Options and Forfeitures**: Forfeitures of stock options directly affect the accounting and financial reporting of common stock options issued by the company.

13. **Performance Goals and Stock-Based Compensation Expense**: Performance goals determine whether stock-based compensation expenses are recognized or reversed, establishing a direct relationship.

14. **Employee Stock Options and Dilution**: Employee stock options are considered in the context of diluted net income, as they may be excluded from calculations due to their anti-dilutive effects.

15. **Revenue Payout Percentage and RSUs**: The Revenue Payout Percentage directly impacts the calculation of Revenue Vesting Eligible Restricted Stock Units (RSUs), affecting employee compensation structures. 

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships and their implications across various business and economic contexts, highlighting how different factors interconnect and influence one another.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the interconnectedness of customer traffic, sales, and economic conditions, particularly in the context of retail.

1. **Customer Traffic and Destination Retailers**: Customer traffic is significantly influenced by the presence of destination retailers, which attract shoppers to shopping areas. This relationship underscores the importance of these retailers in driving foot traffic.

2. **Customer Traffic and Sales**: There is a direct negative correlation between customer traffic and sales. A decrease in customer traffic typically results in lower sales figures, as fewer customers lead to fewer purchases.

3. **Comparable Sales and U.S. Economic Conditions**: Comparable sales are affected by the broader U.S. economic conditions. Economic factors play a crucial role in shaping consumer spending and purchasing behavior, which in turn impacts sales performance.

Overall, the summary emphasizes that destination retailers are vital for enhancing customer traffic, which is essential for maintaining sales levels. Additionally, both customer traffic and comparable sales are sensitive to changes in economic conditions, highlighting the broader economic context's influence on retail performance.

The relationships summarized involve the Annual Report on Form 10-K, the SEC, and the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. 

1. **Annual Report on Form 10-K and SEC**: The Annual Report on Form 10-K is a regulatory document that public companies must file with the SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission) to comply with legal requirements.

2. **Protection under the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995**: The report may contain forward-looking statements that are safeguarded by the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. This protection allows companies to make future projections without the fear of facing litigation, as long as they adhere to the safe harbor provisions outlined in the Act.

Overall, the Annual Report on Form 10-K serves as a crucial tool for regulatory compliance while also providing companies with a framework to communicate future expectations safely.

In summary, the CFO plays a crucial role in financial oversight, while the Conscious Beauty initiative promotes product safety and quality through its certification process. Both entities emphasize accountability and standards in their respective fields.

The COVID-19 pandemic has had a profound impact on various aspects of consumer behavior and product consumption. 

1. **Consumer Behavior**: The pandemic caused significant changes in how consumers behave, primarily due to adaptations required by lockdowns and social distancing measures. This shift reflects a broader transformation in lifestyle and purchasing patterns.

2. **Beauty Products**: The consumption of beauty products was notably affected by the pandemic, as consumer preferences and behaviors evolved in response to the new circumstances brought about by COVID-19. Overall, the relationships highlight the interconnectedness of the pandemic's effects on consumer behavior and specific product categories, illustrating a significant transformation in the marketplace.
community_summary.292:
**Summary of Relationships:**

1. **Net Income and Gross Profit**: Net income is significantly influenced by gross profit, with a notable increase of $676.0 million in gross profit leading to a corresponding rise in net income. This establishes a direct positive relationship where higher gross profit enhances net income.

2. **Net Income and SG&A Expenses**: While net income increased, it was partially offset by a $333.8 million rise in Selling, General, and Administrative (SG&A) expenses. This indicates that increased SG&A expenses have a negative impact on net income, highlighting a balancing effect on overall profitability.

3. **Net Income Calculation**: Net income is derived from gross profit, as it is calculated by subtracting total expenses (including the cost of goods sold) from total revenue. This relationship underscores the foundational role of gross profit in determining net income.

4. **Net Income and Earnings Per Share**: Net income plays a crucial role in the computation of earnings per common share, indicating that fluctuations in net income directly affect the value attributed to each share. This relationship emphasizes the importance of net income in assessing shareholder value.

Overall, the relationships illustrate how gross profit positively influences net income, while SG&A expenses can detract from it, with net income serving as a key metric for evaluating company performance and shareholder returns.

The relationships involving PBSs (Performance-Based Shares) highlight key aspects of their valuation and reporting:

1. **Granting Year**: PBSs were granted in **2021**, which marks the beginning of their performance period.

2. **Valuation Method**: The fair value of the PBSs is determined using a **Monte Carlo simulation**. This statistical method is crucial for accurately assessing the value of these financial instruments, particularly when market conditions are involved.

3. **Reporting Date**: PBSs were reported in financial statements on **January 28, 2023**. This date signifies when the unrecognized stock-based compensation expense related to the PBSs was acknowledged.

Overall, the relationships emphasize the significance of the granting year, the sophisticated valuation technique employed, and the critical reporting timeline for PBSs in financial documentation.

community_summary.315:
**Summary of Relationships Involving Ulta Beauty**

1. **Digital Presence**: Ulta Beauty enhances its digital presence through its website, Ulta.com, which provides online access to products and improves customer engagement.

2. **Adaptation to COVID-19**: In response to the COVID-19 pandemic, Ulta Beauty refreshed its strategic framework to navigate the challenges and ensure ongoing success.

3. **Conscious Beauty Initiative**: Ulta Beauty has established and launched the Conscious Beauty platform to promote sustainable and ethical beauty products, aligning with consumer values and enhancing awareness.

4. **Consumer Research**: The company utilizes consumer research to understand shifts in consumer values and behaviors, informing its strategies for better engagement with customers and the beauty community.

5. **Retail Operations**: Ulta Beauty operates retail outlets across various locations and full-service salons in nearly every store, which are crucial for its business model and customer accessibility.

6. **Product Offering**: Ulta Beauty offers a diverse range of products from over 600 established and emerging beauty brands, catering to various categories and price points.

7. **Collaboration with Brand Partners**: The company collaborates with brand partners to market new and existing products, serving as a significant distribution channel.

8. **Recruitment Support**: Ulta Beauty receives assistance from recruiting specialists to support hiring for its retail and salon operations.

9. **Trademark Registration**: Ulta Beauty has applied for or registered its trademarks in Canada, indicating its expansion and recognition in the Canadian beauty market.

10. **Supply Chain Relationships**: The company sources product components and promotional items from third-party vendors, highlighting its supply chain dynamics.

11. **Regulatory Compliance**: Ulta Beauty must comply with FDA regulations regarding the safety and efficacy of its cosmetic products, which is essential for its operations.

12. **Liability Risks**: There are potential liabilities associated with non-compliance with legal requirements, which could lead to financial consequences.

13. **Manufacturing Relationships**: Manufacturers produce the beauty products sold by Ulta Beauty, which is fundamental to its ability to offer a wide variety of products.

14. **Promotional Events**: Ulta Beauty organizes events like the 21 Days of Beauty to engage customers and boost sales through special offers.

15. **Negligence Risks**: The company faces potential negligence lawsuits related to insufficient label warnings or salon services, emphasizing its responsibility for safety and compliance.

16. **Equity Compensation**: Ulta Beauty issues common stock as part of its equity compensation plans, aligning employee interests with company performance.

17. **Innovation Focus**: The initiative "All Things Beauty" reflects Ulta Beauty's commitment to driving breakthrough and disruptive growth in the beauty sector.

18. **Impact of COVID-19**: The pandemic significantly affected consumer behavior and beauty product consumption, impacting Ulta Beauty's market performance and sales.

19. **Fiscal Operations**: Ulta Beauty operates its business activities within the context of a fiscal year, which is essential for financial reporting and planning.

20. **Loyalty Program Participation**: The company participates in the Ultamate Rewards loyalty program, allowing customers to earn points through their purchases.

This summary encapsulates the key relationships and strategic initiatives of Ulta Beauty, highlighting its adaptability, commitment to sustainability, and focus on customer engagement and innovation.

### Summary of Relationships Involving the Holder

1. **Holder and Shares**: The Holder is entitled to receive dividend equivalents based on the number of Shares corresponding to their outstanding Restricted Stock Units (RSUs). This entitlement is contingent upon specific conditions outlined in the Award Agreement.

2. **Holder and RSUs**: The Holder possesses RSUs and Vesting Eligible RSUs, which are part of their compensation package. They are entitled to these RSUs under the terms of the Award Agreement, and their benefits are contingent upon vesting and employment status.

3. **Holder and CIPCA**: The Holder must consent to and abide by the terms of the Confidentiality, Intellectual Property, and Competitive Activity (CIPCA) agreement to be granted RSUs. This establishes a direct legal relationship between the Holder and the CIPCA.

4. **Holder and Competitive Business**: The Holder is prohibited from engaging with any Competitive Business for two years following termination of service, as defined in the CIPCA. However, they may provide advisory or employment services to such businesses, indicating a complex relationship where their actions can impact these businesses.

5. **Holder and Company**: The Holder is employed by the Company, which manages their employment relationship and benefits. The Holder has tax obligations related to the Company, which is responsible for withholding shares to satisfy these obligations. The Holder must also notify the Company of their intent to retire and provide prior written notice, establishing a communication relationship.

6. **Holder and Administrator**: The Holder must notify the Administrator regarding their retirement intent and provide notice that meets the Administrator's approval, indicating the Administrator's role in the retirement process.

7. **Holder and Qualified Retirement**: The Holder qualifies for Qualified Retirement based on specific conditions, which affects the treatment of their RSUs upon termination of service. Their status as an Employee is also relevant in this context.

8. **Holder and Disability**: The event of Disability can trigger specific provisions regarding the Holder's benefits and may lead to termination of service, affecting their ability to perform their job.

9. **Holder's Participation in the Plan**: The Holder voluntarily participates in the Plan and consents to its provisions by accepting the Restricted Stock Unit Award, indicating their agreement to abide by the terms set forth.

Overall, the relationships highlight the Holder's entitlements, obligations, and the conditions under which they operate within the framework of their employment and associated agreements.

### Summary of Relationships Involving the Company

1. **Insurance and Financial Obligations**:
   - The Company has an **insurance policy** with **UB Insurance, Inc.** that provides coverage against claims.
   - It has **minimum lease payments** of **$91.5 million** for future leases and acknowledges an **unrecognized tax benefit** of **$4.2 million** due to uncertainties in cash flows.

2. **Share Repurchase Programs**:
   - The Company is executing the **2020 Share Repurchase Program** and has implemented the **2022 Share Repurchase Program**, both aimed at buying back its common stock as authorized by the Board of Directors.

3. **E-commerce and Revenue Generation**:
   - The Company engages in **e-commerce sales** and recognizes revenue from customer **points** redeemed through its loyalty program, indicating a direct financial relationship with its customers.

4. **Borrowing and Financial Management**:
   - The Company borrows funds under a **Loan Agreement** with **Wells Fargo Bank** and **JPMorgan Chase Bank**, pledging its assets as collateral. It also manages liabilities related to a **non-qualified deferred compensation plan**.

5. **Operational and Strategic Decisions**:
   - The Company has made commitments to third parties for products and services and has prioritized growth in the **U.S.** while suspending its planned expansion into **Canada** due to pandemic challenges.

6. **Compliance and Regulatory Relationships**:
   - The Company complies with the **Securities and Exchange Commission** by submitting required documents and adheres to the **Dodd-Frank Act**, implementing necessary policies.

7. **Employee Compensation and Incentives**:
   - The Company has adopted an **Equity Incentive Plan** and issues **RSUs** to employees, linking compensation to performance. It also manages tax obligations related to these awards.

8. **Investments and Financial Reporting**:
   - The Company invests in **supply chain optimization** and measures **Loyalty Revenue** as part of its financial reporting, ensuring compliance with accounting standards through implemented policies.

9. **Tax and Financial Reporting**:
   - The Company collects and remits **state sales tax** and records expenses related to its **Deferred Compensation plan** under **SG&A Expenses**, reflecting its financial obligations.

10. **Vendor Relationships**:
    - The Company receives vendor allowances and discounts, indicating a transactional relationship that supports its operational activities.

This summary encapsulates the key relationships and operational dynamics of the Company, highlighting its financial management, strategic initiatives, compliance obligations, and employee engagement strategies.

2. **Senior Leadership Clawback Policy**: This policy is designed to comply with the **Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act**. It aims to establish guidelines for the recovery of compensation from senior leadership in cases of financial misrepresentation, thereby promoting accountability and ethical conduct within organizations.

In summary, both the Registered Public Accounting Firm and the Senior Leadership Clawback Policy are integral to maintaining regulatory compliance, with the former focusing on audit report standards and the latter on executive compensation accountability in line with significant financial legislation.

community_summary.402:
The relationships surrounding Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) highlight their contingent nature and the structured process involved in their awarding, vesting, and conversion into shares. 

1. **Performance Goals**: The earning and vesting of RSUs are directly dependent on achieving specific Performance Goals. This establishes a clear link between performance metrics and the financial benefits employees can receive.

2. **Awarding Process**: RSUs are granted under a defined Plan that outlines the criteria for their awarding, ensuring that employees understand the conditions necessary to receive these financial instruments.

3. **Vesting and Transformation**: Once the specified conditions are met, RSUs transform into Vested RSUs, which can then be sold or transferred. This transformation is a critical step in the lifecycle of RSUs.

4. **Restrictions and Governance**: The RSUs are subject to transfer restrictions as detailed in Section 2 of the governing documents, ensuring that there are clear guidelines on how and when these units can be transferred.

5. **Settlement and Payment**: Upon vesting, the RSUs are settled and paid according to the procedures outlined in Section 5, which describes how employees will receive compensation after their RSUs have vested.

6. **Conversion to Shares**: On the Settlement Date, RSUs convert into Shares, marking the point at which employees actually receive ownership of the shares.

7. **Dividend Equivalents**: RSUs correspond to Dividend Equivalents, which are calculated based on dividends paid on the Shares, linking the financial benefits of RSUs to the performance of the underlying shares.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the structured nature of RSUs, emphasizing their performance-based earning potential, the regulatory framework governing their transfer and settlement, and their eventual conversion into shares that may yield additional financial benefits through dividends.

### Summary of Relationships

1. **Beauty Industry and Environment**: The beauty industry has the potential to positively impact the environment through sustainable practices and social responsibility initiatives.

2. **Infectious Diseases and Natural Disasters**: Natural disasters can worsen the spread of infectious diseases, posing significant risks to public health and safety.

4. **COVID-19 Pandemic and Business**: The COVID-19 pandemic has adversely affected businesses, disrupting operations and negatively impacting financial conditions and profitability.

5. **Epidemics and Business**: Epidemics can materially disrupt business operations and financial health due to increased costs and operational challenges.

6. **Proposition 65 and Healthcare Reform Legislation**: Both Proposition 65 and healthcare reform legislation significantly influence business compliance and operational costs, with changes in one potentially affecting the other.

7. **Restructuring Costs and Canadian Expansion**: The costs related to halting Canadian expansion are part of broader restructuring efforts within the company.

8. **Beauty Category and COVID-19**: The beauty category demonstrated resilience during the COVID-19 pandemic, contributing to an overall increase in sales.

9. **Share Repurchases and Net Cash**: Share repurchases have played a role in increasing net cash used in financing activities, directly impacting the company's cash flow.

10. **Inventory Turns and Operating Income**: Variations in inventory turns can significantly affect operating income, reflecting the efficiency of inventory management and its impact on profitability.

11. **Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax and Consolidated Financial Statements**: The Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax influences the calculations and reporting in consolidated financial statements, affecting the corporation's overall tax obligations.

12. **Employee Benefits and Deferred Tax Assets**: Employee benefits can influence a company's deferred tax assets by potentially leading to future tax deductions, thereby impacting the overall tax position.

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships and their implications across various sectors, highlighting the interconnectedness of environmental, health, and economic factors.

**Summary of Relationships Involving Ulta Beauty, Inc.**

1. **Governance and Oversight**: Ulta Beauty, Inc. is overseen by a Board of Directors, which is responsible for ensuring the company operates in the best interests of its shareholders.

2. **Internal Control Compliance**: The company adheres to the Internal Control - Integrated Framework, which guides its internal control over financial reporting and is subject to audits based on these criteria.

3. **Financial Metrics**: Ulta Beauty, Inc. reports total liabilities and stockholders' equity, reflecting its financial health and capital structure, which includes obligations and equity held by shareholders.

4. **Annual Financial Reporting**: The company reports its financial performance annually, detailing metrics such as net income and cash flows, with specific reports for fiscal years, including fiscal 2022, which covers various financial metrics.

5. **Employee Incentives**: Ulta Beauty, Inc. has established the New Form of Restricted Stock Unit Award Agreement and the Amended and Restated 2011 Incentive Award Plan, which outline compensation and incentive structures for employees.

6. **Corporate Structure**: Ulta Salon, Cosmetics & Fragrance, Inc. operates as a subsidiary of Ulta Beauty, Inc., indicating a corporate relationship where the subsidiary is part of the parent company's business structure.

7. **Financial Relationships**: Ulta Beauty, Inc. has a lending relationship with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A., indicating that it borrows funds as part of its financial operations.

8. **Audit Practices**: The company is audited by an Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm to ensure the accuracy of its financial statements, reinforcing its commitment to transparency and accountability.

This summary encapsulates the key relationships and operational frameworks that define Ulta Beauty, Inc.'s governance, financial practices, employee management, and corporate structure.

**Summary of Relationships Involving Registrant:**

1. **Governance and Compliance**: The Registrant is governed by *The Act*, which mandates adherence to specific regulations, including compliance with Sections 13 and 15(d).

2. **Reporting Obligations**: Under the *Securities Exchange Act of 1934*, the Registrant is required to file reports, establishing a direct regulatory obligation to maintain transparency and accountability.

3. **Data Submission**: The Registrant must submit *Interactive Data Files* as part of regulatory requirements, highlighting the necessity for electronic submission of financial data.

4. **Management Responsibilities**: The Registrant's management is tasked with assessing the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting, a requirement under the *Sarbanes-Oxley Act*, ensuring the integrity of financial disclosures.

5. **Classification**: The Registrant may be classified as a *Shell Company* if it meets the criteria outlined in Rule 12b-2 of the Exchange Act, indicating a lack of significant assets or operations.

6. **Internal Controls Design**: The Registrant is responsible for designing *Internal Control Over Financial Reporting*, which is crucial for ensuring the accuracy of its financial disclosures.

7. **Evaluation by Auditors**: The effectiveness of the Registrant's internal control over financial reporting is evaluated by *Auditors*, ensuring compliance and reliability in financial reporting practices.

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships and responsibilities of the Registrant, emphasizing its regulatory obligations, management duties, and the importance of internal controls and audits in maintaining financial integrity.

5. **Private Label Credit Card vs. Co-branded Credit Card**: Both types of credit cards are financial products offered by retailers, yet they differ in their operational frameworks and partnerships, highlighting the variety of consumer financing options available.

6. **Deferred Tax Assets vs. Deferred Tax Liabilities**: This relationship illustrates the connection between deferred tax assets and liabilities, both of which reflect the tax implications of temporary differences in financial reporting, crucial for assessing a company's overall tax position.

Overall, these comparisons provide valuable insights into customer behavior, financial performance, lease accounting, retail financing options, and tax implications, which are essential for strategic decision-making in retail and finance sectors.
